request.question: During the 2007 Oakland Athletics season, Ken Macha was replaced by a manager who now is the bench coach of what team?

title: 2017 Oakland Athletics season
2007 Oakland Athletics season
Don Wakamatsu
Bob Geren
Steve Staggs
1978 Oakland Athletics season
Rob Thomson
2014 Oakland Athletics season
Roger Smithberg
1980 Oakland Athletics season
sentences: The 2017 Oakland Athletics season was the 50th for the Oakland Athletics franchise in Oakland (all at Oakland Coliseum), as well as the 117th in club history.
The A's began the season on April 3 at home against Los Angeles Angels of Anaheim.
They will finish the season at the Texas Rangers on October 1.
The Oakland Athletics' 2007 season was their first since 2004 in which they defended a title.
The A's were without Frank Thomas (Toronto) and ace Barry Zito (San Francisco), both players who the A's lost to free agency.
They filled the space left by Frank Thomas by signing free agent catcher Mike Piazza to a one-year contract worth $8.5 million to be the team's designated hitter.

They were also without Ken Macha, their former manager who was fired after 4 seasons with the team and replaced by Bob Geren, as well as third-base coach Ron Washington, who accepted the managerial position of the Texas Rangers.
It was their first losing season since 1998.
Wilbur Donald Wakamatsu (born February 22, 1963) is an American former professional baseball player, scout, manager and current coach.
He played as a catcher in Major League Baseball for the Chicago White Sox.
He was hired as bench coach of the Kansas City Royals for the 2014 season.
He was the manager of the Seattle Mariners for the season, as well as the majority of the season.
He was the Toronto Blue Jays' bench coach for 2011 and 2012, after which he was replaced by DeMarlo Hale.
During the 2013 season he worked as a scout for the New York Yankees in the Dallas/Fort Worth area.
Robert Peter Geren (born September 22, 1961) is a former Major League Baseball catcher and manager.
During a 5-year playing career, he played for the New York Yankees (1988–1991) and the San Diego Padres (1993).
He managed the Oakland Athletics from 2007 through 2011 and also coached for the New York Mets.
He is the current bench coach of the Los Angeles Dodgers.
Stephen Robert Staggs (born May 6, 1951) is a former Major League Baseball second baseman who played for two seasons.
He played 72 games for the Toronto Blue Jays during the 1977 Toronto Blue Jays season and 47 games for the Oakland Athletics during the 1978 Oakland Athletics season.
The 1978 Oakland Athletics season was the team's eleventh in Oakland, California.
The team sought to rebound from its first losing season in a decade (a 63-99 result in 1977).
Despite low expectations, the Athletics remained competitive for nearly three-quarters of the season.
Despite posting a respectable 61-56 mark through 117 games, the Athletics collapsed in the season's final weeks; their 8-37 finish ensured a second consecutive season of fewer than 70 wins.
Robert Lewis Thomson (born August 16, 1963) is a Canadian bench coach for the New York Yankees.
He previously served as bench coach during the 2008 season, third base coach from 2009 - 2014, and returned to serving as bench coach in 2015.
He has also served as Major League Field Coordinator for the organization.
The 2014 Oakland Athletics season was the 46th for the franchise at O.co Coliseum, as well as the 114th in club history.
The Athletics entered the season hoping to win a third consecutive American League West championship; to that end, the team made a number of key signings and trades during the 2013-14 MLB offseason.
Notably, Athletics traded outfielder Michael Choice for left fielder Craig Gentry and pitcher Josh Lindblom; they also traded the promising but oft-injured Brett Anderson for reliever Drew Pomeranz.
Additional trades brought in relievers Fernando Abad (acquired for John Wooten), Luke Gregerson (acquired for Seth Smith), and Jim Johnson (acquired for Jemile Weeks and David Freitas).
In free agency, the Athletics signed former All-Star starting pitcher Scott Kazmir to a two-year deal.
These moves, among others, sought to bolster the depth of team's starting pitching and bullpen.
Roger Craig Smithberg (born March 21, 1966) is a former Major League Baseball pitcher who played for two seasons.
He pitched for the Oakland Athletics for 13 games during the 1993 Oakland Athletics season and two games during the 1994 Oakland Athletics season.
The 1980 Oakland Athletics season was the team's thirteenth season in Oakland.
The A's, under first-year manager Billy Martin, began the season with low expectations following their insipid 1979 campaign.

Strong performances from pitchers Mike Norris, Matt Keough, and Rick Langford, along with the brilliant play of breakout star (and future Hall-of-Famer) Rickey Henderson, paved the way for a staggering 29-win increase over the previous year's output.
The Athletics, only one year removed from baseball's worst record, swung to a second-place finish behind their 83-79 record.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Produce a single, concise, structured first-hop evidence report as the `summary`. The summary must do all of the following and only include content that helps determine what to fetch next:

- Enumerate concrete facts found in the provided passages. For each fact include the passage source label and either a very short quoted fragment (up to about 15 words) when a decisive short fact appears or a brief paraphrase otherwise. Keep each fact to one short line.

- Explicitly identify the bridge entity(ies) and any missing or ambiguous fact(s) that prevent answering the question from the given passages. If a name or identifier in the question corresponds to multiple candidate pages present in `passages`, list every candidate variant and mark the name as ambiguous; do not choose between them.

- Prescribe concrete, prioritized next-hop retrieval targets to resolve each missing or ambiguous element. For each target, specify which page(s) or disambiguation page to fetch and what exact section, keyword, or datum to extract (for example: specific field such as birth year, office held, list membership, dates, or the section heading to check). Prioritize targets (1, 2, 3...) when helpful.

- Only produce a final answer in `summary` if every required fact for every entity is present and unambiguous in the supplied passages; otherwise do not produce a final answer and focus on retrieval guidance.

Formatting and content constraints:
- Keep the summary concise (a short paragraph or a few short bullet-like lines).
- Produce only the single output field `summary` and nothing else.
- Preserve source-linked evidence; do not assert facts without a passage label.
- Do not invent facts or assume which ambiguous candidate is intended.
- Avoid copying long verbatim spans; use short quoted fragments when decisive and paraphrase otherwise.
- When an expected authoritative page is missing from `passages`, note that absence and include that page title as a next-hop retrieval target.

Input: fields `question` and `passages`. Output: one field `summary` meeting the requirements above.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

You are to produce exactly one compact second-hop retrieval query that targets the missing bridge or supporting evidence referenced by summary_1. Follow these operational rules when constructing the single `query` string:

- Use visible anchor tokens from `question` or `summary_1` (named works, titles, artist names, years or other identifiers only if present) and append a short retrieval cue naming the bridge sought (e.g., artist, album, recording, genre, season count, passage, supporting quote).
- Emit one concise line (one string assigned to `query`), roughly ≤ 10–12 words, formatted either as a short interrogative that names the anchors or as a compact keyword string (anchors + bridge keywords).
- If summary_1 explicitly requests a missing attribute or passage, emit the focused retrieval query rather than asking for clarification.
- Do not include answers, conclusions, enumerations, provenance notes, speculative language, or user-facing explanations; do not invent entities or add tokens that are not present in the inputs.
- Include year or other identifier tokens only if they appear in the provided inputs and help disambiguate.
- Keep wording retrieval-oriented and terse. Preserve the fallback that if retrieval using this query later fails, downstream steps may request clarification.

Output contract: produce exactly one field `query` whose value is the single compact retrieval query line described above.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions for producing `summary` (single field only):
1. Merge the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into one compact integrated `summary` that preserves the primary answer name(s) and only includes passage-supported facts needed to justify or disambiguate that answer.
2. If `context` already contains a concise answer (a short sentence or a name), keep that content as the first sentence of `summary` when it is consistent with facts in `passages`. If `context` lacks a concise answer, create a concise first sentence that names the primary answer(s), using the canonical labels from `context` when available and otherwise from `passages`.
3. Expand the summary only when `passages` provide explicit, matching second-hop evidence about one or more named entities referenced in `context`. When expansion is appropriate, append at most one short supporting sentence per named entity. Each supporting sentence must begin with the entity's full name as shown in the inputs and paraphrase up to a few core attributes that are directly present in the `passages` (examples: nationality, a notable award/status, a current affiliation/team, a birth date, membership/partnership relation, or an explicit founding/formation fact).
4. Keep `summary` concise: typically 1–2 sentences for a single entity; allow up to 3 sentences only when multiple distinct named entities each require one supporting sentence. Prioritize the facts most directly necessary to answer the `question`.
5. Only assert facts that are directly supported by the provided `passages` or `context`. Do not introduce new entities, dates, awards, affiliations, roles, or causal claims that are not present in the inputs.
6. Prefer paraphrase over long verbatim copying. Use short quoted phrases only when the exact short phrase (roughly ≤10 words) appears in the `passages` and quoting preserves clarity.
7. If `context` and `passages` conflict, prefer facts that are explicitly present in the `passages`: update the concise answer to match passage-supported content rather than preserving a contradicted `context`.
8. If the retrieved `passages` do not supply explicit supporting evidence for named entities in `context`, keep the `summary` concise and do not expand or speculate.
9. Output exactly one field named `summary` whose value is the integrated post-retrieval summary text. Do not output additional fields, metadata, or commentary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Follow these operational rules when producing `answer`:
- Use only the content of `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2` as your evidential basis. Do not introduce new facts or external information.
- If both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary (yes/no) conclusion to the question, set `answer` to exactly one lowercase token: yes or no. The `answer` field must contain nothing else (no punctuation, capitalization, quotes, explanation, or additional text).
- Decide which token to emit by aggregating the visible summaries only. Treat `summary_2` as the later integrated evidence; do not let unresolved language in `summary_1` alone prevent emitting the single-token result if both summaries now unambiguously agree.
- If the summaries conflict, are ambiguous, incomplete, hedged such that the relation is unresolved, or if the question is non-binary, do not apply the single-token rule; instead follow the agent's existing final-answer fallback behavior for unresolved or non-binary cases (which may produce a fuller response or an uncertainty signal).

Produce only the `answer` field as specified.

response.hop1_query: During the 2007 Oakland Athletics season, Ken Macha was replaced by a manager who now is the bench coach of what team?
hop1_docs: 2007 Oakland Athletics season | The Oakland Athletics' 2007 season was their first since 2004 in which they defended a title.  The A's were without Frank Thomas (Toronto) and ace Barry Zito (San Francisco), both players who the A's lost to free agency.  They filled the space left by Frank Thomas by signing free agent catcher Mike Piazza to a one-year contract worth $8.5 million to be the team's designated hitter.  They were also without Ken Macha, their former manager who was fired after 4 seasons with the team and replaced by Bob Geren, as well as third-base coach Ron Washington, who accepted the managerial position of the Texas Rangers.  It was their first losing season since 1998.

Ken Macha | Kenneth Edward Macha ( ; born September 29, 1950, in Monroeville, Pennsylvania) is a former Major League Baseball third baseman and manager.  He managed the Oakland Athletics from 2003–2006, including American League Western Division championships in both his first and final seasons with the team, and later managed the Milwaukee Brewers (2009–10).

Bob Geren | Robert Peter Geren (born September 22, 1961) is a former Major League Baseball catcher and manager.  During a 5-year playing career, he played for the New York Yankees (1988–1991) and the San Diego Padres (1993).  He managed the Oakland Athletics from 2007 through 2011 and also coached for the New York Mets.  He is the current bench coach of the Los Angeles Dodgers.

Vern Hoscheit | Vernard Arthur Hoscheit (April 1, 1922 – June 11, 2007) was an American professional baseball catcher, coach and manager.  He served as a coach on four World Series championship Major League Baseball teams with the Oakland Athletics and the New York Mets.  Hoscheit was the Mets' bullpen coach from 1984 to 1987, which included their World Series run in 1986.  During that season's spring training, Hoscheit accurately predicted that the team would clinch the National League East Division title on September 17, long before any regular season game was even played.

2010 Arizona Diamondbacks season | The Arizona Diamondbacks' 2010 season, the franchise's 13th season in Major League Baseball, included the team's attempt to make the playoffs for the first time since 2007.  On July 1, 2010, the Arizona Diamondbacks fired Manager A. J. Hinch following a 31-48 start to the 2010 season and promoted Kirk Gibson who had served as the bench coach of the team since the 2007 season.  Along with the dismissal of Hinch came the firing of General Manager Josh Byrnes.

List of Oakland Athletics managers | The Oakland Athletics are a professional baseball team based in Oakland, California.  Before moving to Oakland in 1968, the team played in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania from 1901 through 1954 and in Kansas City, Missouri from 1955 through 1967.  The Athletics are members of the American League (AL) West division in Major League Baseball (MLB).  In baseball, the head coach of a team is called the manager, or more formally, the field manager.  The duties of the team manager include team strategy and leadership on and off the field.  The team has employed 30 different managers in its history.  The current Athletics' manager is Bob Melvin.

Don Wakamatsu | Wilbur Donald Wakamatsu (born February 22, 1963) is an American former professional baseball player, scout, manager and current coach.  He played as a catcher in Major League Baseball for the Chicago White Sox.  He was hired as bench coach of the Kansas City Royals for the 2014 season.  He was the manager of the Seattle Mariners for the season, as well as the majority of the season.  He was the Toronto Blue Jays' bench coach for 2011 and 2012, after which he was replaced by DeMarlo Hale.  During the 2013 season he worked as a scout for the New York Yankees in the Dallas/Fort Worth area.
response.summary_1:
- [1] "replaced by Bob Geren" — Ken Macha was replaced by Bob Geren during the 2007 Oakland A's season.
- [3] "is the current bench coach of the Los Angeles Dodgers." — Bob Geren is the bench coach of the Los Angeles Dodgers.
- Bridge entity: Bob Geren (unambiguous in passages [1] and [3]).
- Final answer: Bob Geren is the bench coach of the Los Angeles Dodgers (supported by [3]).
response.hop2_query: Bob Geren bench coach Los Angeles Dodgers
hop2_docs: Bob Geren | Robert Peter Geren (born September 22, 1961) is a former Major League Baseball catcher and manager.  During a 5-year playing career, he played for the New York Yankees (1988–1991) and the San Diego Padres (1993).  He managed the Oakland Athletics from 2007 through 2011 and also coached for the New York Mets.  He is the current bench coach of the Los Angeles Dodgers.

1996 Los Angeles Dodgers season | The 1996 season saw the Dodgers remain atop the standings most of the season.  However, longtime manager Tommy Lasorda suffered a heart attack in mid-season and had to step down.  Bill Russell, Lasorda's bench coach and a former Dodger player, was chosen to manage the rest of the season.

2001 Los Angeles Dodgers season | The 2001 season saw Jim Tracy take over as the Manager, after serving as the Bench coach the previous two seasons.  The Dodgers won 86 games, finishing third in the Western Division of the National League, six games behind the eventual World Series champion Arizona Diamondbacks.  This was their last season to be broadcast by KTLA (5).

List of Los Angeles Dodgers coaches | The following is a list of coaches, including position, year(s) of service(s), who appeared at least in one game for the Los Angeles Dodgers National League franchise also known previously as the Brooklyn Dodgers.

Bob Lee (baseball) | Robert Dean "Bob" Lee (born November 26, 1937) is an American former closer and spot starter in Major League Baseball who played from 1964 through 1968 for the Los Angeles Dodgers, Los Angeles/California Angels, and the Cincinnati Reds.  Listed at 6 ft , 225 lb , Lee batted and threw right handed.  He was nicknamed ″Moose″, ″Horse″ and ″Big Bob″.

1978 Los Angeles Dodgers season | The 1978 season ended with the Los Angeles Dodgers winning their second straight National League pennant and losing to the New York Yankees in the World Series again.  Dodger coach Jim Gilliam died at the end of the season and his uniform number was retired by the team prior to Game 1 of the World Series.

Roy Campanella Award | The Roy Campanella Award is given annually to the Los Angeles Dodgers player who best exemplifies the spirit and leadership of the late Hall of Fame Brooklyn Dodger catcher, Roy Campanella.  The award is voted on by all Los Angeles Dodgers uniformed personnel, players, and coaches.  The award has been given out since 2006.
hop2_titles: List of Los Angeles Dodgers coaches
response.summary_2: Bob Geren is the bench coach of the Los Angeles Dodgers. Bob Geren (Robert Peter Geren) is a former Major League Baseball catcher who managed the Oakland Athletics from 2007 through 2011.
